touch_lab_readme_txt.content: This folder is ready for touch practice.

mkdir-one-folder.title: Create one folder with `mkdir`
mkdir-one-folder.bodyMarkdown:
Use `mkdir` when you need a new folder in the current location.

```bash
mkdir class-notes
```

Then check with:

```bash
ls
```

If you see `class-notes`, the folder exists.

mkdir-p-nested.title: Create nested folders with `mkdir -p`
mkdir-p-nested.bodyMarkdown:
Sometimes you need a folder inside another folder. `mkdir -p` creates the parent folders too.

```bash
mkdir -p project-kit/src project-kit/docs
```

You can also do this in separate commands. The important result is the folder structure, not one perfect command line.

touch-files.title: Create empty files with `touch`
touch-files.bodyMarkdown:
`touch` creates an empty file when the file does not exist.

```bash
touch class-notes/todo.txt
```

An empty file is still useful as a placeholder. In the next lesson, you will put real text into files with `echo`.
